axis: PSI log analyzer + conservative per-host threshold recommendation

predecessor.instrumentation: scripts/bin/hexa_remote PSI block emits TSV to $HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_LOG (default /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log)
predecessor.tsv_format: ISO_ts \t host \t psi_avg10 \t threshold \t outcome \t reason

outcome_split.note: row count 80 reflects accumulation between ω-network-2 instrumentation and ω-network-3 analysis

log_state.preliminary_observation: 100% reject on hetzner+htz and 50% on ubu2 corroborate ω-network-2 hypothesis that hetzner-class hosts are running near saturation under recent workload; ubu1 conversely is idle (max 0.03). HOWEVER all per-host counts are < 50 — recommendations DEFERRED.

subcommands_or_flags.(default): human-readable table + machine-parseable TSV summary block
subcommands_or_flags.--recommend: ALSO emit RECOMMEND lines (conservative; only suggests when count >= 50)

recommendation_policy.tight_rule: suggested = round(p95 + 5); never below current threshold; cap at 95
recommendation_policy.rationale: Conservative — single-cycle data accumulation insufficient; suggestions only fire once per-host has >=50 samples to avoid spurious tuning from short bursts.

edge_cases.expected: no data: log file not found
edge_cases.actual: no data: log file not found (/tmp/psi_missing_xxx.log)

synthetic_test_results.verdict: All 5 cases PASS. Stats math verified by hand on synthetic data; boundary heuristics fire as designed.

operator_loop: 1. Allow log to accumulate (passive — emitted by hexa_remote on every PSI probe)
4. Review RECOMMEND lines — sanity-check against operational knowledge (e.g. hetzner saturation is real, not artifact)
5. If suggestion accepted, manually export per-host env (e.g. HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD_HETZNER=92) — wiring deferred to ω-network-4
workflow_documentation.current_recommendation_status: ALL hosts return KEEP/insufficient_data — no operator action required this cycle. Re-run analyzer when log >= ~200 rows (≈50/host).
workflow_documentation.log_rotation_note: No rotation implemented. If log grows unbounded, operator may truncate via ': > /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log' or use logrotate; defer to ω-network-4 if it becomes an issue.
workflow_documentation.preliminary_signal_for_ops: Even with sparse data, hetzner/htz showing 100% reject ratio is a strong qualitative signal worth investigating BEFORE waiting for full calibration — likely a workload mismatch (heavy-compute forced onto hetzner per ω-network-2 audit) rather than a threshold-tuning problem.

next_cycle.trigger: per-host count >= 50 in /tmp/hexa_remote_psi.log AND analyzer --recommend produces non-KEEP suggestions for >=2 hosts
scope: Add HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_THRESHOLD_<HOST_UPPER> env-var lookup in scripts/bin/hexa_remote (per ω-network-2 next_step plan)
Optional: per-host config file at scripts/etc/hexa_remote_psi_thresholds.conf (KEY=VAL) sourced before threshold check
Optional: log rotation policy (size-based truncate at 10MB OR weekly rotate)
Optional: investigate hetzner-saturation root cause (workload distribution, not threshold) — this may be ω-network-5 territory if asymmetry is workload-driven
next_cycle.blockers: Conservative gate (count >= 50) may need 1-2 weeks to satisfy under current probe cadence.

rollback.blast_radius: ZERO — analyzer is read-only over the TSV log. No edits to hexa_remote, no docker rebuild, no daemon. Removing the file removes only operator tooling.
rollback.side_effects: NONE — synthetic test logs created in /tmp were cleaned up; HEXA_REMOTE_PSI_LOG env var was only set inline for tests.

constraints_honored.session_hexa_only: analyzer is shell+awk (per spec: 'analyzer can be hexa or shell'); no live ssh probes initiated
